draft_received.description:
Repair input received, initial candidate family resolved via
resolveInitialFamilyCandidate (clean-family-repair.ts:186).

evaluating_attempt.description:
Executing runCleanFamilyRepairAttempt for the current candidate.
The attempt updates the output and returns one of:
family_ready | needs_scope_clarification | needs_semantic_clarification
| blocked | suggested_family.
The first four are terminal; suggested_family loops back here with
a new candidate.

attempt_suggested_family.description:
Cyclic transition: the attempt returned suggested_family. The
orchestrator (target code) updates candidate, increments
attempts_count, then re-enters evaluating_attempt. POM models the
cycle structurally; MAX_FAMILY_REPAIR_ATTEMPTS = 3 is enforced
in target code only.

family_ready.description:
Repair terminated with a coherent family-resolved output. The
analyzer's family_enforcement step has succeeded.

needs_scope_clarification.description:
Repair terminated declaring that scope clarification is needed.
The analyzer will surface this to the operational FSM as a
clarification stop-exit.

needs_semantic_clarification.description: Repair terminated declaring that semantic disambiguation is needed.

blocked.description:
Repair terminated with a block (e.g., terminal_block_locked or a
block guard fired on every candidate).

exhausted.description:
MAX_FAMILY_REPAIR_ATTEMPTS = 3 attempts consumed without a final
outcome; buildCleanFamilyRepairExhaustedOutput produces the
fallback output.

pom.description:
Clean family repair sub-FSM, invoked synchronously by the analyzer
FSM's family_enforcement state. Source:
pipeline/analyzer/clean-family-repair.ts.

The repair iterates up to MAX_FAMILY_REPAIR_ATTEMPTS = 3 times. Each
attempt evaluates a candidate family via runCleanFamilyRepairAttempt:
- if the attempt returns a final outcome (family_ready,
  needs_scope_clarification, needs_semantic_clarification, blocked),
  the loop ends and the repair terminates with that outcome;
- if the attempt returns a "suggested_family", the candidate is
  updated and the loop continues.
If the budget of MAX_FAMILY_REPAIR_ATTEMPTS attempts is consumed
without a final outcome, the repair terminates as "exhausted".

POM cannot enforce the retry budget structurally (open point: bounded-
loop primitive). The cycle is modeled; the bound lives in target
code.

events.name: start_attempt
events.description: Begin or resume the repair loop with the current candidate.

events.name: attempt_final_family_ready
events.description: Attempt produced a coherent family_ready outcome.

events.name: attempt_suggested_family
events.description: Attempt did not finalize; suggested a different candidate. Loop.

events.name: budget_exhausted
events.description: MAX_FAMILY_REPAIR_ATTEMPTS reached without a final outcome.

invariants:
Each call to runCleanFamilyRepairAttempt produces exactly one
outcome: one of five terminals or a suggested_family that re-
enters the loop. The five-way fan-out + one cycle is faithful to
the source's resolveOutcome (clean-family-repair.ts:650+).

The cycle on evaluating_attempt is bounded by
MAX_FAMILY_REPAIR_ATTEMPTS in target code. POM cannot enforce
this bound; it is declared in the description and metadata. Same
open-point class as the parse_retry / coherence_retry cycles in
the parent analyzer FSM.

tried_families is accumulated across attempts in target code and
surfaced only at the exhausted terminal. The intermediate
bookkeeping is not part of the POM context_schema, consistent with
the "no shared state during invocation" pillar.
open_points:
Bounded retry counter (same class as analyzer-fsm parse_retry /
coherence_retry). Source: MAX_FAMILY_REPAIR_ATTEMPTS = 3
(clean-family-repair.ts:122). POM models the cycle, not the bound.
Concrete schema-growth candidate: loop_guard { max_visits: 3,
on_exhaustion: { exit_target: exhausted } } on the
evaluating_attempt state.
The five output_by_terminal payloads share a common "resolved_
output" + "attempts_count" prefix; the differentiating fields
are the per-terminal extras (clarification_text, ambiguity_kind,
block_reason, fallback_output, tried_families). POM v1 expresses
this by repetition; an opt-in `output_common:` shared section
could de-duplicate, but it is purely cosmetic.
Source's CleanFamilyRepairTrace (attempts, finalOutcome,
finalFamily, maxAttempts) is observable diagnostic data. POM
captures finalOutcome (terminal_state) and attempts_count, not
the per-attempt trace. The trace lives in target code under the
"documentation, not runtime" stance.
